hypothesis: B1/B10/B11 all used 4 power iterations to find the dominant active-subspace direction, unchanged from the original B1 experiment. B12's failure mode (a deflated second direction carried ~no exploitable signal beyond noise) reconfirmed the covariance is strongly rank-1 dominated, which means power iteration should converge in far fewer than 4 steps. Verified directly first: on 5 real dataset MLPs, cosine similarity of the direction after 2 iterations to a 6-iteration reference was >=0.9986 in every case (1 iteration was riskier, dropping to 0.90 worst case). Reducing to 2 iterations should halve that phase's call count (256->128) at zero raw-FLOP cost per call, unlike B11's full-Jacobian materialization which traded call count for bigger, costlier calls and barely moved effective_compute.

evaluation.command: uv run --frozen whest run --estimator <estimator> --runner subprocess --dataset hf://aicrowd/arc-whestbench-public-2026@v1-phase1 --split mini --flop-budget 272000000000 --format json

reports.note: Champion report reused byte-identical from the B4 experiment (same estimator.py@1598169, same deterministic dataset/seeds); candidate report freshly executed.

pre_implementation_validation.method: Loaded 5 real dataset MLPs (indices 0,1,2,5,10) directly via whestbench.load_dataset/iter_mlps, computed the soft-gate power-iteration direction at 1..6 iterations, measured cosine similarity of each to the 6-iteration direction.
pre_implementation_validation.result: After 2 iterations: cosine similarity 0.99857-0.99999 across all 5 MLPs (worst case MLP index 5: 0.998567). After 1 iteration: 0.90116-0.99957 (worst case MLP index 5: 0.901160, materially less converged). Chose 2 iterations as the safe reduction.

aggregate.candidate_vs_B10_note: B10 (4 iterations) final_layer_mse was 7.995433086876e-06. B13 (2 iterations) is 7.931290535907e-06 -- marginally BETTER, not worse, confirming 2 iterations does not sacrifice direction quality for this dataset.

paired.lineage_comparison: paired_mean_delta across the B1/B10/B11/B13 lineage: B1=1.966e-07, B10=8.176e-08, B11=9.179e-08, B13=6.391e-08 (this run) -- the smallest yet, and CI is narrower than B10's on the upper end (3.722e-07 vs 3.903e-07) though the lower bound moved slightly more negative (-2.444e-07 vs -2.268e-07, still comfortably straddling zero either way).

overhead_diagnostic.comparison: B10 (4 power iterations, 353 calls): ratio 1.278 (aggregate mean). B13 (2 power iterations, 225 calls): ratio 1.266. Halving the power-iteration call count (256->128, a 36% cut in total calls: 353->225) only reduced the overhead ratio by about 1 percentage point -- power iteration is not the only remaining source of overhead; the 32 still-unbatched diagonal soft-gate matmuls are untouched and now make up a larger fraction of the remaining gap. Champion's reference ratio is ~1.186-1.19.

reason: The paired 95% CI [-2.444e-07, 3.722e-07] is not entirely below zero, so this does not clear the promotion gate. But this is real, incremental progress in the B1/B10/B11 lineage: the smallest paired_mean_delta yet (6.391e-08), confirmed accuracy did not degrade (final_layer_mse marginally improved vs. B10, validating the 2-iteration direction-convergence check), and a modest overhead reduction (ratio 1.278->1.266) achieved without B11's costly full-materialization tradeoff. The overhead reduction was smaller than the 36% call-count cut might suggest, indicating the remaining ~32 unbatched diagonal soft-gate calls are now a larger relative share of the gap to the champion's ~1.19 ratio.
